title: Sandbox Isolation
subtitle: Sandbox Isolation
description: How to isolate the execution environment when Agent runs code to prevent host machine pollution. Balancing security and convenience in code execution environments.
sub_problems: Code execution isolation: User code runs in sandbox without affecting host machine
File system isolation: Agent can only access designated directories
Network isolation: Restrict Agent's network access scope
Resource limits: Constraints on CPU, memory, and execution time
Environment consistency: Alignment between sandbox environment and target environment
Cross-platform command filtering: Single blacklist must cover POSIX (rm -rf) and Windows (del /f) command variants
Dual-system independent defense: MCP Server and Agent framework each implement security checks, maintaining consistency
Import control: Restrict modules that code can import, preventing dangerous API calls
Intent-to-code isolation: LLM converts user intent to code before execution, avoiding direct injection
Cross-process sandbox discovery: Multiple worker processes need to find existing sandboxes in the same thread, avoiding duplication
Multi-runtime adaptation: Same code must work across macOS Apple Container, Docker, and K8s runtimes
Kernel crash recovery: How to quickly rebuild Jupyter Kernel and restore execution context after crash
Edit syntax protection: Code edits generated by LLM may introduce syntax errors, requiring lint comparison and automatic rollback
Output truncation strategy: Success output takes head, error output takes tail; different truncation directions affect LLM understanding
Configuration drift detection: Perceive and fix inconsistencies between running container configuration and expected configuration
Browser isolation: Browser controlled by Agent requires independent network and authentication mechanism
Tool access control: Fine-grained allow/deny policies for tools available to Agent in sandbox
best_practices: Dual-layer abstraction is best practice: Provider manages lifecycle, Sandbox manages operations; DeerFlow's pattern is worth learning
Virtual paths must translate bidirectionally: Input translation + output reverse translation, otherwise Agent sees real paths and gets confused
Lazy initialization saves resources: Not all Agents need sandbox; create only on first tool call
Set execution timeout: MiroThinker 600s, blog scenarios 30s, preventing infinite loops from exhausting resources
Defend against LLM hallucination: INVALID_SANDBOX_IDS blacklist is simple and effective defense
Result truncation protection: Auto-truncate output exceeding threshold, avoiding token explosion
Regex blacklist superior to string matching: \brm\s+-[rf]{1,2}\b matches rm -rf variants, avoiding simple 'in' check bypass
Configuration-driven isolation switch: restrict_to_workspace allows disabling during development, enabling in production for convenience and security
AST static analysis import whitelist is zero-overhead pre-execution security check, suitable for supplementing process isolation
Configuration loading priority chain (environment variables > module config > global config > defaults) lets same codebase adapt to multiple deployment environments
LLM intent→code conversion is safer than direct user input execution; ToolAgent's _generate_code_from_intent pattern is worth learning
Deterministic ID is key to cross-process discovery: sha256(thread_id) lets all processes derive same sandbox identifier
Three-layer consistency retrieval (process cache → state store → backend discovery) balances performance and correctness
Skill directory read-only mount (read_only=True) prevents sandbox code from tampering with shared resources
Select isolation level by role: lightweight scripts use subprocess, interactive analysis uses Jupyter Kernel, production deployment uses Docker
Add lint guard to edit operations: flake8 before/after comparison, auto-rollback on new syntax errors, preventing LLM from breaking code
Output truncation must distinguish direction: success takes head (useful info first), failure takes tail (error info last)
Configuration hash detection for drift: SHA256(normalize(config)) compares container label, cold containers auto-rebuild
Hot container protection window: recently used containers don't auto-rebuild, avoiding interruption of running tasks
Environment variable three-layer filtering: regex blacklist + value validation (null byte/overlength/base64) + optional strict whitelist
Atomic registry write: temp file + rename prevents concurrent write corruption, write lock prevents race conditions
Symlink escape protection: realpathSync resolves symlinks then validates blacklist again

values.DeerFlow: Provider+Sandbox dual-layer abstraction (Local subprocess / Aio Docker)
values.DeepCode: Process-level soft isolation: subprocess + Path.resolve() boundary check, no container
values.DeepTutor: Process-level: subprocess.run + tempfile temporary directory, production stacks Docker container
values.MetaGPT: Three-layer progression: subprocess process-level → Jupyter Kernel-level → Docker container-level
values.OpenClaw: Docker container-level: read-only root + cap-drop ALL + no-new-privileges + independent browser container

values.DeepCode: No virtual path translation, direct real path usage + startswith prefix check
values.DeepTutor: No virtual path translation, direct real path usage with allowed_roots whitelist validation
values.MetaGPT: WorkspaceConfig unified workspace path, SWE_CMD_WORK_DIR restricts operation scope
values.OpenClaw: Bidirectional translation: host↔container paths via mount point matching, supports workspace/agent/bind three mount sources

values.DeerFlow: SandboxMiddleware lazy initialization + idle timeout reclamation 600s
values.DeepCode: No explicit lifecycle, workspace persists with process, tempfile auto-deleted after use
values.DeepTutor: Lazy initialization + TemporaryDirectory context manager auto-cleanup
values.MetaGPT: ExecuteNbCode three-state management build/terminate/reset, DataInterpreter explicit terminate on completion
values.OpenClaw: Configuration hash-driven rebuild + JSON registry persistence + auto-prune idle 24h/alive 7 days

values.DeepCode: Dual defense: MCP string blacklist + Nanobot regex blacklist + path traversal detection
values.DeepTutor: Three-layer defense-in-depth: ImportGuard(AST) + path whitelist + subprocess timeout
values.MetaGPT: forbidden_commands command blacklist + flake8 lint guard edit rollback + output truncation prevent token overflow
values.OpenClaw: 5-layer defense-in-depth: bind blacklist + symlink escape protection + env sanitization + network/seccomp/apparmor validation

values.DeepCode: No auto-repair, execute_python failure then LLM iterates code changes
values.DeepTutor: ToolAgent detects exit_code failure then adds error prefix hint for path correction
values.MetaGPT: edit_linting.sh before/after flake8 comparison, new syntax errors auto-rollback to backup
values.OpenClaw: Hot container protection: no auto-rebuild within 5-minute window, prompt CLI command for manual operation

values.DeepCode: Workspace level, all tools share same workspace boundary
values.DeerFlow: Thread-level isolation: each thread_id has independent workspace/uploads/outputs three directories
values.MetaGPT: Role-based layering: Engineer uses subprocess, DataInterpreter uses Kernel, SWE-Agent uses Terminal

values.DeepCode: Configuration-driven: restrict_to_workspace switch unified control file and shell tools
values.DeerFlow: Tool-layer unified entry: all 5 @tool functions gated through ensure_sandbox_initialized
values.MetaGPT: SWE-Agent command whitelist (open/edit/search/submit), Terminal forbidden_commands blacklist
values.OpenClaw: glob pattern allow/deny lists, agent > global > default three-level priority

values.DeepCode: Not applicable: MCP Server and Nanobot are independent processes, each manages workspace
values.DeerFlow: Deterministic ID (sha256(thread_id)[:8]) + FileSandboxStateStore + fcntl file lock

values.DeepCode: Supports Docker Compose deployment, but sandbox logic doesn't distinguish runtimes
values.DeerFlow: Auto-detect Apple Container / Docker, K8s via RemoteSandboxBackend + Provisioner
values.MetaGPT: subprocess local execution + Docker container deployment, no native K8s/Apple Container support

values.DeepCode: deny_patterns covers both POSIX and Windows command patterns
values.MetaGPT: Terminal distinguishes win(cmd.exe)/unix(bash), forbidden_commands unified interception

solutions.title: SandboxProvider ABC + Virtual Path Mapping + Middleware Lifecycle
solutions.description: Classic Provider + Sandbox dual-layer abstraction: SandboxProvider(ABC) manages sandbox lifecycle (acquire/get/release), Sandbox(ABC) provides 5 tools (bash/ls/read_file/write_file/str_replace). LocalSandbox uses subprocess + local FS (global singleton), AioSandbox uses HTTP API → Docker/Apple Container. Virtual path system translates /mnt/user-data/workspace/* bidirectionally (tool layer replace_virtual_path + sandbox layer _resolve_path + output reverse translation _reverse_resolve_paths_in_output). SandboxMiddleware controls lazy initialization (lazy_init=True defers to first tool call), AioSandbox supports deterministic ID sha256(thread_id)[:8], idle timeout reclamation 600s, atexit graceful shutdown.
design_philosophy: Dual-layer abstraction: Provider manages lifecycle (acquire/get/release), Sandbox manages execution operations (5 tools), clear separation of concerns
Virtual path unified perspective: Agent always sees /mnt/user-data/* virtual paths, bidirectional translation (tool layer + sandbox layer) + output reverse translation
Lazy initialization: SandboxMiddleware lazy_init=True defers acquire to first tool call, avoiding resource waste for Agents not needing sandbox
Configuration-driven switching: sandbox.use field in config.yaml dynamically loads implementation class via resolve_class() reflection
migration_scenarios: Agent projects requiring code execution isolation (5 sandbox tools plug-and-play)
Multi-tenant scenarios needing per-thread independent sandbox (AioSandbox deterministic ID + idle reclamation)
Progressive deployment from LocalSandbox in dev to AioSandbox in production (one-line config.yaml switch)

solutions.title: E2B Code Interpreter Cloud Sandbox + LLM Auto-Repair
solutions.description: python_mcp_server.py provides cloud code execution environment via e2b_code_interpreter.Sandbox. Core constants: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=600s single execution timeout, MAX_RESULT_LEN=20000 character result truncation, MAX_ERROR_LEN=4000 character error truncation. INVALID_SANDBOX_IDS blacklist (default/sandbox1/sandbox/some_id/new_sandbox) prevents LLM from fabricating sandbox IDs. Supports file upload to sandbox. Each task gets independent Sandbox instance without interference. Migration adaptation as CodeValidatorService: timeout reduced to 30s, result truncation reduced to 5000 characters, validate_and_fix() auto-repairs code on validation failure (max 2 attempts).
design_philosophy: Cloud isolation: E2B sandbox completely isolated, independent Sandbox instance per task
Defensive design: INVALID_SANDBOX_IDS blacklist prevents LLM hallucination generating invalid IDs
Result truncation protection: 20K character output truncation + 4K error truncation, avoiding token explosion
Validate-repair closed loop: validate_and_fix() validation failure → error feedback to LLM → regenerate → revalidate
migration_scenarios: Production environments requiring cloud-based strong isolation code execution
Blog/tutorial generation scenarios where code examples need runnable verification
Scenarios requiring LLM auto-repair of code errors (validate_and_fix pattern)

solutions.title: Workspace Path Validation and Command Blacklist Sandbox
solutions.description: DeepCode implements process-level soft isolation through validate_path boundary checking + deny_patterns regex command blacklist, with dual-layer independent defense in MCP Server and Nanobot.
design_philosophy: Path boundary validation: validate_path ensures all file operations stay within workspace
Regex command blacklist: deny_patterns covers command variants across platforms
Dual-layer defense: MCP Server and Nanobot each implement independent security checks
migration_scenarios: Projects needing lightweight process-level isolation without container overhead
Scenarios with dual-system architecture requiring consistent security enforcement

solutions.title: Path Whitelist Approach
solutions.description: Path whitelist: only allow access to predefined directory list, all path operations validated against whitelist first; default read-only: file system defaults to read-only, writes require explicit authorization to specific directories.
design_philosophy: Whitelist-based access control: explicit allow model more secure than blacklist
Default deny principle: read-only by default, write requires explicit grant
migration_scenarios: High-security scenarios requiring strict file system access control
Multi-tenant environments needing strong isolation between users

solutions.title: Process-Level Workspace Isolation
solutions.description: WorkspaceManager manages isolated workspace, all code executes in temporary directory under data/user/run_code_workspace/ (src/tools/code_executor.py:115-244). Each execution gets fresh temporary directory, automatic cleanup after completion.
design_philosophy: Temporary workspace per execution: fresh environment prevents state leakage between runs
Automatic cleanup: TemporaryDirectory context manager ensures resource cleanup
Workspace hierarchy: organized directory structure for workspace/uploads/outputs
migration_scenarios: Educational platforms requiring isolated code execution per student
Scenarios needing automatic resource cleanup without manual intervention

solutions.title: Three-Layer Code Execution Isolation
solutions.description: MetaGPT implements three-layer isolation by role: subprocess process-level (RunCode 10s timeout), Jupyter Kernel-level (ExecuteNbCode 600s + three-state lifecycle), Docker container-level deployment, combined with SWE-Agent command whitelist and flake8 lint guard.
design_philosophy: Role-based layering: different roles use appropriate isolation levels
Progressive isolation: lightweight scripts use subprocess, interactive analysis uses Kernel, production uses Docker
Lifecycle management: three-state (build/terminate/reset) for Kernel execution
Lint guard protection: flake8 before/after comparison prevents syntax error introduction
migration_scenarios: Projects with diverse execution requirements across different roles
Scenarios needing progressive isolation from development to production
Environments requiring code quality validation before execution

solutions.title: Docker Container Sandbox
solutions.description: Configuration hash-driven container rebuild: SHA256 hash detects configuration changes, cold containers auto-rebuild, hot containers prompt manual rebuild (docker.ts:419-494); multi-layer security validation: bind mount path blacklist + symlink escape protection + network mode/seccomp enforcement.
design_philosophy: Configuration hash detection: SHA256 hash identifies drift between running and expected state
Hot container protection: recently used containers protected from auto-rebuild to avoid interruption
Multi-layer security: bind blacklist + symlink protection + network/seccomp validation
Atomic operations: temp file + rename prevents concurrent write corruption
migration_scenarios: Production environments requiring strong container-level isolation
Scenarios with frequent configuration changes needing automatic drift detection
Multi-tenant deployments requiring strict resource and network isolation
